label: SoC-integrated NPU
note: conv-native NPU sharing the SoC's LPDDR. Closer to the bank tail than a pure dataflow NPU — Gather and ArgMin are in the op-set, so a k=1 nearest-neighbour is nearly feasible on-device, but TopK is not, and the fp32 bank is not native; the tail is still a host residue.
op_support_provenance: COMPILE-VERIFIED (rknn-toolkit2 2.3.2 simulator, no board): conv graph all ops -> NPU; bank tail Conv1x1 -> NPU while TopK and ArgMin -> CPU (host residue confirmed). NUANCE: a bare scaled-dot-product attention block fuses to the NPU (exSDPAttention), so 'transformer_attention: unsupported' is a DETECTOR-level verdict (Point-MAE's FPS/kNN tokenizer stays host), not a claim that no attention op exists. See deploy/compile_ops.json / bead j5s.5.

instances.name: rknn_rk3588
instances.label: Rockchip RK3588 RKNN NPU
instances.ext_memory: shared system LPDDR4/4X/5 (+ ~956 KiB dedicated NPU SRAM per IP)
instances.sources: S9 (6 TOPS int8, triple NPU), S10 (1 MiB SRAM / 956 KiB per IP + shared LPDDR)
instances.note: 6 TOPS int8 (triple NPU core). Gather/ArgMin on-device but TopK unsupported; k=1 argmin closer to feasible, fp32 bank still not native. Dedicated SRAM is tiny (~956 KiB); the working set lives in shared LPDDR.
